posts.text:
¡Por fin!!!!


𝙟𝙤𝙨𝙚 𝙯𝙖𝙢𝙤𝙧𝙖 (@jczamora)

#𝗭𝗮𝗺𝗼𝗿𝗮𝗟𝗶𝗯𝗿𝗲

Hoy, tras más de 1,295 días de detención arbitraria, mi papá, el periodista #JoseRubénZamora, recupera su libertad. ¡Defensa en libertad!

Gracias a todas y todos por acompañarnos. Su apoyo y solidaridad han sido fundamentales. Gratitud total.





— https://nitter.net/jczamora/status/2022047898800971966#m

posts.text:
Fuel shortages, blackouts, and growing uncertainty.

After the U.S. attack on Venezuela, Donald Trump imposed a strict blockade on oil shipments cutting off a major source of fuel for Cuba and worsening the island’s already deep economic crisis. The president insists the Cuban regime will “fall soon.” But on the ground, the reality is far more complex.

Many Cubans want change as the humanitarian crisis deepens, yet at the same time, fear what U.S. intervention could mean for their future.

On t

posts.text:
[Español abajo]

After President Trump has insisted that the Cuban regime that has governed the island for more than six decades will “fall soon,” many in Latin America have kept a close watch over what could happen in the near future in Cuba. 

To go over recent developments, @JorgeRamosNews and @PaoRamos speak to The Miami Herald’s Cuba policy reporter, Nora Gámez Torres. And in the second half of this episode, they’re joined by Jon Lee Anderson, a staff writer for The New Yorker who reported

posts.text:
“Political amnesia” doesn’t happen in a vortex. It’s enacted by forces in power. 

Director Kleber Mendonca Filho joins #TheMoment this week to discuss his Oscar-nominated film @thesecretagentfilm, set in a coastal Brazilian city at the height of Brazil’s military regime in 1977.

Listen now and catch new episodes every Wednesday on the free @iHeartRadio app, YouTube, or wherever you get your podcasts. 

—

La “amnesia política” no pasa en el vacío. La provocan quienes están en el poder.

El dir
